channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: These Catholic sisters want to start a new order with a difficult mission: helping clergy abuse survivors
item.description: Sisters Theresa Alethia and Danielle Lussier call themselves the Sisters of the Little Way of Beauty, Truth and Goodness. Their order plans to help victims of abuse.
item.content:encoded:
Sisters Theresa Alethia and Danielle Lussier call themselves the Sisters of the Little Way of Beauty, Truth and Goodness. Their order plans to help victims of abuse.

(Image credit: Natosha Via for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Adora Namigadde

item.title: How predominantly Black areas in 6 states have been split up by redistricting
item.description: These maps show that the redistricting battle President Trump initiated has so far had a particular impact on largely Black congressional districts in Republican-led states.
item.content:encoded:
These maps show that the redistricting battle President Trump initiated has so far had a particular impact on largely Black congressional districts in Republican-led states.

(Image credit: Rahul Mukherjee)
item.dc:creator: Rahul Mukherjee

item.title: The White House pushes healthy eating. It cut a landmark nutrition education program
item.description: Nutrition advocates and public health experts are mystified by the decision to eliminate SNAP-Ed, given the Trump administration's embrace of the Make America Healthy Again agenda.
item.content:encoded:
Nutrition advocates and public health experts are mystified by the decision to eliminate SNAP-Ed, given the Trump administration's embrace of the Make America Healthy Again agenda.

(Image credit: Rachel Wisniewski for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez

item.title: DOT cranks up its campaign to strip bike lane references from federal websites
item.description: Transportation officials have removed a growing list of safety materials that mention bike lanes from federal websites. Safety advocates warn that's undermining proven measures for reducing crashes.
item.content:encoded:
Transportation officials have removed a growing list of safety materials that mention bike lanes from federal websites. Safety advocates warn that's undermining proven measures for reducing crashes.

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: After deadly shootings, police experts question how well ICE officers are vetted
item.description: The recent fatal shootings by ICE officers in Texas and Maine come at a time when the federal agency has hired a huge number of officers quickly. Some law enforcement experts worry that this kind of a hiring spree can lead police agencies to cut corners in vetting new hires.
item.content:encoded:
The recent fatal shootings by ICE officers in Texas and Maine come at a time when the federal agency has hired a huge number of officers quickly. Some law enforcement experts worry that this kind of a hiring spree can lead police agencies to cut corners in vetting new hires.

(Image credit: Gregory Rec)
item.dc:creator: Meg Anderson

item.title: A town renamed its festival 'AI Love Irondale Day.' Then came the comments
item.description: The online outcry over the renaming of a summer festival illustrates the unease over the data center boom in the South.
item.content:encoded:
The online outcry over the renaming of a summer festival illustrates the unease over the data center boom in the South.

(Image credit: Stephan Bisaha)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: FDA panel considers easing peptide restrictions
item.description: Advisers to the Food and Drug Administration are weighing the evidence for seven different peptides. Favorable votes would pave the way for easier access.
item.content:encoded:
Advisers to the Food and Drug Administration are weighing the evidence for seven different peptides. Favorable votes would pave the way for easier access.

(Image credit: Sarah Silbiger)
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: 'New York Times' alleges White House abuses legal system to intimidate reporters
item.description: The New York Times will argue in court Thursday that the White House is abusing the justice system to intimidate reporters over a story that angered President Trump.
item.content:encoded:
The New York Times will argue in court Thursday that the White House is abusing the justice system to intimidate reporters over a story that angered President Trump. 

(Image credit: BRENDAN SMIALOWSKI/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump administration signs nuclear cooperation agreement with Saudi Arabia, Trump ramps up aggression against Iran despite declining support, House passes Pentagon funding bill.
item.content:encoded: Trump administration signs nuclear cooperation agreement with Saudi Arabia, Trump ramps up aggression against Iran despite declining support, House passes Pentagon funding bill.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: OpenAI blamed a hacking event on its AI models gone rogue. Here is what to know
item.description: The incident is stirring debates over the need for stronger AI guardrails and the extent to which AI agents are capable of acting on their own.
item.content:encoded:
The incident is stirring debates over the need for stronger AI guardrails and the extent to which AI agents are capable of acting on their own.

(Image credit: Michael Dwyer)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
